skill.name: skill-builder
skill.description: Create new Claude Code Skills with YAML frontmatter, progressive disclosure structure, validation checklists, and organized skill directories. Use when designing custom skills, documenting workflows, or learning the Claude Skills format.
skill.summary: Build well-structured Claude Code Skills with templates, examples, and validation guidance.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown documentation examples rather than executable skill code. Shell commands, hidden paths, relative links, and URLs are used to teach skill structure and installation, with no evidence of malware, exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported shell execution patterns appear inside fenced Markdown examples that teach users how to create or run optional skill files. The skill package contains only SKILL.md and does not include executable scripts that run automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are embedded in documentation code fences and are clearly presented as examples. No separate executable file or automatic command invocation was found.

low_findings.title: Hidden Claude Skill Directory References
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE with minor user-awareness risk. The hidden home-directory paths point to the documented Claude personal skill location. They can write files if a user follows the tutorial, but this is expected behavior for a skill-building guide.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are limited to ~/.claude/skills and .claude/skills examples. I found no attempt to access secrets, shell profiles, SSH keys, or unrelated hidden files.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The URLs are placeholder resources or official/community documentation links in a Learn More section. No network request code or data transfer behavior is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear as Markdown links, not as fetch, curl, or webhook calls. Their context is instructional documentation rather than exfiltration or command-and-control.

low_findings.title: Relative Related-Skill Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The ../ sequences are Markdown links to neighboring example skills. They are not used in file reads, writes, archive extraction, or command arguments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The traversal markers are visible only in related-skill hyperlinks. There is no code path that dereferences user-controlled paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts From Textual Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak-cryptography findings appear to match prose, filenames, or Markdown examples such as SKILL.md and README.md references. No hashing, encryption, signing, or password-storage implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced areas are metadata, Markdown filenames, or templates. I found no semantic evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: Creating reliable AI skills requires the right file structure, metadata, and usage guidance. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a practical framework for drafting custom skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains required SKILL.md YAML frontmatter fields and formatting rules.
Shows personal and project skill directory layouts for Claude Code.
Describes progressive disclosure for metadata, main instructions, and referenced files.
Provides minimal, intermediate, and advanced skill templates.
Includes validation checklists for structure, content quality, scripts, and testing.
Lists examples for documentation and code generation skills.
limitations: Does not include an automated generator script in the package.
Examples must be adapted before use in a real project.
Guidance is focused on Claude skill conventions and may not fit every agent platform.
Does not validate generated skills against a live marketplace schema.

use_cases.title: Create a Personal Workflow Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated workflow into a structured Claude Code skill with clear triggers and setup instructions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Skill Templates
use_cases.description: Define shared project skill layouts with documentation, resources, scripts, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Teach Skill Authoring Practices
use_cases.description: Use examples and checklists to explain how progressive disclosure keeps skill context focused.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic Claude Code skill for [workflow]. Include the name, description, folder structure, and a concise SKILL.md outline.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Skill Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill idea and write better YAML frontmatter. Make the description include what it does and when Claude should use it: [idea].

prompt_templates.title: Design Progressive Disclosure
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a multi-file skill for [domain]. Separate core instructions, reference docs, examples, resources, and optional scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Skill Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this SKILL.md draft for structure, trigger clarity, progressive disclosure, file references, script safety, and missing validation steps: [draft].

output_examples.input: Create a skill for generating release notes from merged pull requests.
output_examples.output: A skill outline with a clear release-notes trigger, required frontmatter, suggested docs, example resources, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Review my skill description for autonomous matching.
output: A shorter description that starts with the main action.
A when-to-use clause with concrete trigger phrases.
Notes about vague wording to remove.

output_examples.input: Plan an advanced skill with scripts and templates.
output_examples.output: A recommended directory structure, script purpose list, resource organization plan, and checklist for testing the skill before publishing.

best_practices: Keep the main SKILL.md focused on core instructions and common workflows.
Use specific trigger words in the description so the agent can match the skill reliably.
Move long examples, schemas, and advanced references into separate files.
anti_patterns: Using vague descriptions that do not state when the skill should be used.
Putting large reference material directly in SKILL.md when a linked file would work better.
Adding scripts without explaining their purpose, inputs, and expected outputs.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create Claude Code Skills with proper metadata, structure, examples, and validation guidance.

faq.question: Does it generate files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides instructions and templates that an agent or user can follow to create files.

faq.question: Can it help with project-specific team skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains project skill locations, shared documentation patterns, and version-controlled skill structures.

faq.question: Does it support skills with scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents how to reference optional scripts and resources, but it does not include runnable scripts itself.

faq.question: Why is progressive disclosure important?
faq.answer: It keeps frequently loaded context small while leaving detailed references available when needed.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found documentation examples but no executable package code, malware behavior, or prompt injection evidence.
